metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: Wiz data connector with template version 4.0.0

connectorUiConfig.title: Wiz for Microsoft Sentinel
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Wiz integration pushes Wiz **Issues**, **Detections**, and **Audit Logs** into Microsoft Sentinel in real time. Wiz sends data to a Data Collection Endpoint using the Wiz service principal, so there is no Azure Function to host and no shared keys to manage. This connector grants that service principal the least-privilege RBAC it needs; Wiz then creates the data collection rule and tables and begins pushing once you configure the integration in the Wiz portal.

value:
union isfuzzy=true (WizIssuesV3_CL), (WizDetectionsV3_CL), (WizAuditLogsV3_CL)
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(30d)

dataTypes.name: WizIssuesV3_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
WizIssuesV3_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: WizDetectionsV3_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
WizDetectionsV3_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: WizAuditLogsV3_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
WizAuditLogsV3_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

customs.name: Subscription permissions
customs.description: **Owner** or **User Access Administrator** at the subscription is required to create the custom role definition (Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/write).

customs.name: Microsoft Entra permissions
customs.description: Permission to read (and, if the Wiz enterprise application is not yet consented, create) the Wiz service principal in Microsoft Entra ID.

instructionSteps.description:
ARM cannot resolve a Microsoft Entra object, so you supply the Wiz service principal **object ID**. In Azure Cloud Shell (no local install required; creating the service principal needs permission to register enterprise applications in Microsoft Entra ID) run the line below — it prints the object ID, creating the service principal first if it does not yet exist in your tenant:

```bash
az ad sp show --id ae69b94b-e367-4660-9b35-cfb4b5966728 --query id -o tsv 2>/dev/null \
  || az ad sp create --id ae69b94b-e367-4660-9b35-cfb4b5966728 --query id -o tsv
```
instructionSteps.title: STEP 1 - Get the Wiz service principal object ID

instructionSteps.description:
Deploy the custom role and assignment into the resource group that hosts this workspace. Paste the object ID from Step 1 (and, optionally, a Data Collection Endpoint resource ID).

1. Click **Deploy the Wiz RBAC role**: 

	[![Deploy the Wiz RBAC role](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2FAzure%2FAzure-Sentinel%2Fmaster%2FSolutions%2FWiz%2FData%20Connectors%2Fazuredeploy_WizSentinelIntegration.json) 
2. Select the **Subscription** and the **Resource group** that hosts this workspace. 
3. Enter **wizPrincipalObjectId** (from Step 1); optionally **dataCollectionEndpointId**. 
4. **Review + create**.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 2 - Grant Wiz the RBAC

instructionSteps.description: In Wiz, go to **Settings > Integrations > Add > Microsoft Sentinel**. Paste your **Tenant ID**, **Workspace Resource ID**, and **Data Collection Endpoint Resource ID**, tick *Deployment completed*, and save. Wiz then creates the data collection rule and tables and begins pushing Issues, Detections, and Audit Logs into this workspace.
instructionSteps.title: STEP 3 - Configure the integration in Wiz

connectorUiConfig.title: Wiz for Microsoft Sentinel
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Wiz integration pushes Wiz **Issues**, **Detections**, and **Audit Logs** into Microsoft Sentinel in real time. metadata.description:
A visualized overview of Wiz Issues, Detections and Audit Logs.
Explore, analyze and learn about your security posture using the Wiz Findings overview

@{workbookKey=WizFindingsWorkbook; logoFileName=Wiz_logo.svg; description=A visualized overview of Wiz Issues, Detections and Audit Logs.
Explore, analyze and learn about your security posture using the Wiz Findings overview; dataTypesDependencies=System.Object[]; dataConnectorsDependencies=System.Object[]; previewImagesFileNames=System.Object[]; version=3.0.0; title=Wiz Findings overview; templateRelativePath=WizFindings.json; subtitle=; provider=Wiz}.description

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Wiz solution for Microsoft Sentinel lets Wiz push Wiz Issues, Wiz Detections, and Wiz Audit Logs into Microsoft Sentinel in real time. Wiz sends data to a Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) using the Wiz service principal; this solution grants that service principal the least-privilege RBAC it needs, and Wiz then creates the data collection rule and tables. There is no Azure Function to host and no workspace shared keys to manage.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  1. Data Collection Rules and the Logs Ingestion API

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
